name: Osseo Brooklyn School Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Regan family (operating as American Student Transportation / Northstar Bus Lines)
principals: Robert J. Regan (founder, 1963); Patrick O. Regan; Matthew Regan
districts_or_customers: Osseo Area Schools (Dist. 279) and other Minneapolis-area public and charter districts; sister co. Northstar serves Twin Cities
recent_news: In 2024 Patrick O. Regan filed STB notice to acquire control of multiple MN motorcoach/school bus operators (Faribault Transportation, Minnesota Coaches, Marschall Line, Rehbein Transit, Minn-Dakota Coaches, Voyageur Bus, Ready Bus) - consolidating a growing Regan-family MN platform; operating as American Student Transportation / Northstar with 200+ buses and electric bus rollout.
fit_notes: Third-gen family operator but actively CONSOLIDATING - Patrick Regan is the acquirer of multiple MN operators in 2024. More likely a competitor/regional platform than a Summit tuck-in target; low succession likelihood.

name: Mcgough Bus Co Inc
principals: Henry McGough (founder, 1977); MaryAnn McGough (Exec Secretary/Treasurer); Meghan McGough Paster (COO)
districts_or_customers: South Jersey districts; two hubs in Sewell and Camden, NJ; serves Glassboro area
recent_news: NJBIA profile (Henry McGough): started 1977 with $1k savings + $10k loan from parents and one bus/one route; grown to 135 vehicles across two SJ hubs; remains family-run with founder and daughter as COO.
fit_notes: Solid 2nd-gen NJ family operator with ~135 buses in attractive S. Jersey market. Founder is aging and 2nd gen daughter active - reasonable tuck-in candidate; Summit already in NJ via Durham footprint.

name: Cottage Hill Co
owner_or_parent: independent (Peacock family; affiliated with Kabert Industries / Beck Bus Transportation)
principals: Robert (Bob) Peacock (President, owner since 1968); Robert Beck Jr. (COO, Kabert email); Kathy Martino (VP Operations)
districts_or_customers: Addison, Elmhurst, Lombard, Villa Park, Westmont - DuPage County/Chicago western suburbs; regular and special-ed contracts
recent_news: Bob Peacock has owned and grown the business for 48+ years (since 1968); affiliated with Kabert Industries and Beck Bus Transportation Corp. (Illinois UST records show Beck Bus as owner of tanks). 2025 revenue ~$5.5M.
fit_notes: Classic succession-ready target: family-owned 75+ yrs, single owner (Peacock) at 48+ yrs at helm, multi-district DuPage Co. presence. High tuck-in fit IF Kabert/Beck Bus affiliated entities can be acquired together; warrants direct outreach.

name: Kickert School Bus Lines
owner_or_parent: First Student, Inc. (Cincinnati, OH) - acquired Feb 2026 from Cook-Illinois Corp. (Benish family)
principals: Previously John Benish Sr./Jr. (Cook-Illinois); founders Richard & Bessie Kickert (1915)
districts_or_customers: 10+ south suburban Cook County communities; ~12,000 students; 2.5M annual miles; based in Lynwood, IL (Bolingbrook is satellite/secondary)
recent_news: Sold to First Student in Feb 2026 (per HF Chronicle 2/13/2026), via parent Cook-Illinois Corp. Previously a Cook-Illinois subsidiary since 1980 when John Benish Sr. bought it from Bill Messmaker. 100-yr milestone in 2015.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET - acquired by First Student (EQT-owned strategic competitor) in Feb 2026. Was previously owned by Cook-Illinois Corp. since 1980. Remove from pipeline.

name: Mid Placer Transportation
owner_or_parent: Joint Powers Authority (government entity) owned by member school districts
principals: Martin J Ward (CEO/Director); JPA governed by member-district board
districts_or_customers: Member districts: Ackerman Charter SD, Alta-Dutch Flat ESD, Colfax ESD, Loomis Union SD, Placer Union HSD (Placer County, CA)
recent_news: Mid-Placer is a JPA created in 1979 by member Placer County school districts to consolidate home-to-school and special-needs transport. Operates as a public/government agency, not a private contractor.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET - public Joint Powers Authority (government entity) owned by member school districts. Not acquirable as a private contractor. Remove from pipeline.
